Home Reference

HTTP Status Codes Reference

HTTP status codes are issued by a server in response to a client request. They are grouped into five classes by their first digit. The table below lists the codes you will meet most often when debugging web applications, together with their meaning and the typical situations that produce them.

CodeNameMeaning
200OKThe request succeeded
301Moved PermanentlyThe resource has a new permanent URL
403ForbiddenThe server refuses to authorize the request
404Not FoundThe resource does not exist
503Service UnavailableThe server is temporarily overloaded

Classes at a glance

  • 1xx — informational responses
  • 2xx — success
  • 3xx — redirection
  • 4xx — client errors
  • 5xx — server errors

When in doubt, remember that 4xx means the client should change something about the request, while 5xx means the server failed to fulfil a valid request.
